content:
I'm just trying to use vec! in some driver code and getting

error: cannot find macro vec in this scope
--> drivers/gpu/drm/nova/bios.rs:254:16
|
254 | let bit_vec = vec![0xff, 0xb8, 'B', 'I', 'T'];
| ^^^

is this available in the kernel or do I need some magic?
That macro uses infallible allocations, which are not allowed in the kernel.
thanks, makes sense why I can use it then!
yeah it's just 5 bytes vec, so I'll just figure it out
